 ## Files

-| `test.yml`             | TypeScript test workflow                                                                                                                               | Modifying TS tests                                                 |
-| `test-integration.yml` | Integration test workflow                                                                                                                              | Modifying integration tests                                        |
-| `main-workflow.yml`    | Orchestrates TS workflows                                                                                                                              | Understanding TS CI structure                                      |
+| File                   | What                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | When to read                                                             |
+| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `main-workflow.yml`    | Pull-request orchestrator for TypeScript work. On PRs to `dev`/`main`, calls `build.yml`, `lint.yml`, `test.yml`, and `test-integration.yml`. It does **not** call `gitleaks.yml`.                                                                                                                 | Understanding PR CI structure                                            |
+| `build.yml`            | Reusable TypeScript build/typecheck workflow. Uses the local setup action, then runs `bun run check-types` and `bun run build`.                                                                                                                                                                    | Modifying TS build or package build behavior                             |
+| `lint.yml`             | Reusable TypeScript formatting/lint workflow. Uses the local setup action, then runs `bun run format` and `bun run lint`.                                                                                                                                                                          | Modifying TS lint or formatting behavior                                 |
+| `test.yml`             | Reusable unit/coverage workflow. Uses the local setup action, runs the tokenlists fixture guard, then runs `bun run test` and `bun run test:cov`.                                                                                                                                                  | Modifying TS unit tests or coverage behavior                             |
+| `test-integration.yml` | Reusable integration-test workflow. Uses the local setup action and runs `bun run test:integration`.                                                                                                                                                                                               | Modifying integration tests                                              |
+| `rust.yml`             | Rust CI for `services/` and `crates/`: `cargo check --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo sqlx prepare --workspace --check`, and `cargo test --workspace --lib` with `SQLX_OFFLINE=true`. Triggered by path-filtered PRs or reusable calls from deploy caller workflows. | Adding Rust CI steps, debugging Rust failures                            |
+| `deploy.yml`           | Reusable Azure Container Apps deploy. Builds 4 images, resolves/pins digests, updates 5 present Container Apps including `fiat-server-worker`, and optionally pins/runs `fiat-webhook-sync`.                                                                                                       | Modifying deployment steps, adding secrets, changing image tagging       |
+| `deploy-staging.yml`   | Main-branch staging deploy. On push to `main`, runs `rust.yml` first, then calls `deploy.yml` with `environment: staging`.                                                                                                                                                                         | Changing staging deployment triggers or GitHub Environment usage         |
+| `deploy-nlayer.yml`    | Nlayer deploy. On push to `nlayer-env`, runs `rust.yml`, calls `deploy.yml` with `auto_webhook_sync: true`, then records a GitHub Deployment for the `nlayer` environment.                                                                                                                         | Changing nlayer deployment, webhook-sync behavior, or deployment records |
+| `gitleaks.yml`         | Reusable secret-scanning workflow. Installs Gitleaks `8.30.0` and runs `gitleaks detect --source . --verbose --redact`. Currently `workflow_call` only; no orchestrator invokes it.                                                                                                                | Wiring or debugging secret scanning                                      |
+
+## Referenced Local Action
+
+`build.yml`, `lint.yml`, `test.yml`, and `test-integration.yml` call `./.github/actions/setup`. That composite action installs Bun `1.2.2`, Node `24`, Foundry nightly, runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, then runs `bun run build`.

@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ Landmines worth knowing on day one — each has burned someone before.
 -   **Rust builds use SQLx offline mode by default.** The repo commits cached query metadata under `.sqlx/`, so `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check`/`cargo build` works without a live database — this is what CI does. When you **add or modify** a `query!`/`query_as!` macro, you must regenerate the offline cache or CI's offline build will fail on the new query: with `DATABASE_URL` pointing at a live DB whose schema matches your macros, run `cargo sqlx prepare --workspace` (from the `sqlx-cli` crate), then commit the updated `.sqlx/`. **A plain `cargo build` with `DATABASE_URL` set validates queries online but does not write the cache** — `prepare` is the regeneration step. For end-to-end local dev (Docker, Prisma migrations, the Rust services, Ponder, frontend, Iron webhook PATCH), use `bun run dev:stack` (= `./scripts/dev-stack.sh`). `bun run dev` only spins up JS/TS workspaces; Turbo cannot see Rust.
 -   **Iron API host is `iron.xyz`.** The legacy `getiron.com` host has been dead since 2026-04-08 — it returns TLS errors. Sandbox base is `https://api.sandbox.iron.xyz/api`.
 -   **Stealth privacy invariants:**
-    -   Stealth send is **USDC-only by config**, not by contract. The `STEALTH_CHAIN_CONFIGS` tables in `apps/defi-app/src/lib/config/stealth-config.ts` only list USDC entries (with `aaveForwardingSupported: true`). The `/v2/send` flow gates on `isStealthSupportedChain` + `isStealthAutoDepositSupported`; both derive from those tables. (There is a defined-but-unused `isStealthSupportedToken` in the same file — don't be misled by it; nothing imports it.)
+    -   Stealth send is **USDC-only by config**, not by contract. The `STEALTH_CHAIN_CONFIGS` tables in `apps/defi-app/src/lib/config/stealth-config.ts` only list USDC entries on Sepolia (11155111), Base Sepolia (84532), and Base mainnet (8453). `aaveForwardingSupported` is `true` on Sepolia and Base mainnet but `false` on Base Sepolia until the forwarder is deployed there (TODO DEF-548 in the file). The `/v2/send` flow gates on `isStealthSupportedChain` + `isStealthAutoDepositSupported`; both derive from those tables. (There is a defined-but-unused `isStealthSupportedToken` in the same file — don't be misled by it; nothing imports it.)
     -   **Never put `viewTag` in a Ponder GraphQL `where:` clause.** Filtering server-side by view tag would leak the recipient's viewing-key-derived byte to the indexer operator and break stealth privacy. **Selecting** `viewTag` in the response is fine — and necessary — because the client filters locally; that's what the comment `// stored, NEVER filtered server-side` on `viewTag` in `indexers/announcement/ponder.schema.ts` is reminding you. Today every FE query follows this: `apps/defi-app/src/lib/modules/stealth/subgraph-client.ts#ANNOUNCEMENTS_QUERY` only filters on `blockNumber_gte` + `chainId`. Ponder's generated schema technically exposes `viewTag`, `viewTag_in`, etc. as filter args by default — no caller uses them, but if you ever add a new query, do not introduce a `viewTag*` filter (use a non-leaky predicate or filter client-side).
 -   **Webhook endpoints must verify HMAC-SHA256 signatures, enforce a 5-minute timestamp tolerance (`TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS = 300`), and persist the inbound `event_id` with a uniqueness constraint** to defend replay. Both Iron (`services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs`) and Rain (`rain_webhooks.rs`) follow this shape today; new webhook endpoints inherit the obligation.

-## Overview
-
-This repository is a monorepo consisting of 2 packages and 1 app:
+# DeFi-com Monorepo
+
+A non-custodial DeFi wallet with first-class **stealth address (ERC-5564)** support, **passkey-based authentication** (WebAuthn → ZeroDev ERC-7579 Kernel accounts), integrated **fiat on/off-ramps** via Iron, and **debit cards** via Rain. The codebase is a **hybrid TypeScript + Rust monorepo** orchestrated by Bun + Turbo (TS) and Cargo (Rust).
+
+> Looking for the architecture deep dive? See [`docs/architecture.md`](./docs/architecture.md). For repository invariants and contributor guidance, see top-level [`CLAUDE.md`](./CLAUDE.md).
+
+---
+
+## Workspace layout
+
+```text
+monorepo/
+├── apps/
+│   ├── defi-app/           SvelteKit 2 + Svelte 5 PWA — the product
+│   ├── docs/               Vocs-powered docs site (React 19)
+│   ├── tradfi-server/      TypeScript TradFi API (Bun + Hono, port 3002, partially recovered)
+│   ├── fiat-server/        Empty post-cleanup (legacy JS removed in a54c3905) — canonical at services/fiat-server
+│   └── passkey-server/     Empty post-cleanup (legacy JS removed in a54c3905) — canonical at services/passkey-server
+├── packages/               19 TypeScript libraries (account, common, config, contracts, database,
+│                           defi-aggregator, defi-id, iron, kernel-stealth-address-sdk, merkle-scripts,
+│                           passkeys, rain, stealth-address-sdk, stocks, tokenlists, tokens, ui-kit, …)
+├── services/               Rust microservices (Axum + SQLx)
+│   ├── fiat-server/        Iron + Rain proxy (port 3001)
+│   ├── passkey-server/     WebAuthn RP (port 8080)
+│   └── ptp-server/         Identity & reputation (port 3004) — built/deployed out of band
+├── crates/                 Rust shared workspace crates
+│   ├── db/                 SQLx repository layer (compile-time query verification)
+│   ├── mq/                 Message-queue abstraction (azqueue / nats / servicebus, feature-gated)
+│   ├── iron-client/        Typed Iron API client
+│   └── rain-sdk/           Rain SDK (workspace member; replaces the deleted rain-client)
+├── indexers/
+│   ├── announcement/       Ponder stealth-announcement indexer
+│   └── pnl/                PnL indexer
+├── infra/                  OpenTofu modules for Azure (Front Door, APIM, Postgres, Redis, ACR, Container Apps)
+├── docs/                   Markdown setup guides + runbooks (architecture, integrations, Azure, stealth)
+├── package.json            Bun workspace + Turbo task graph
+├── turbo.json              JS/TS task graph (Turbo doesn't see Rust services)
+└── Cargo.toml              Rust workspace
+```
 
--   [`@ts-turborepo-boilerplate/contracts`](./packages/contracts): A library for writing all required smart contracts
--   [`@ts-turborepo-boilerplate/sample-lib`](./packages/sample-lib): A sample library for querying account balances
--   [`@ts-turborepo-boilerplate/sample-app`](./apps/sample-app): A demo sample app that uses the sample-lib

+## Available scripts
 
-## Available Scripts
+`package.json` defines the workspace task graph through Turbo. Notable entries:
 
-### `create-package`
+| Script                                   | What it runs                                                                                     |
+| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `dev`                                    | `turbo run dev --concurrency=24` (TS/JS workspaces only)                                         |
+| `dev:stack`                              | `./scripts/dev-stack.sh` — full stack (Docker + Rust + Ponder + FE)                              |
+| `dev:stack:no-indexer`                   | Same as `dev:stack`, skipping the Ponder indexer                                                 |
+| `dev:rust`                               | **Deprecated alias** — prints a warning then delegates to `dev:stack`. Use `dev:stack` directly. |
+| `build`                                  | Workspace build via Turbo                                                                        |
+| `check-types`                            | `tsc --noEmit` across all TS packages                                                            |
+| `lint` / `lint:fix`                      | ESLint                                                                                           |
+| `format` / `format:fix`                  | Prettier                                                                                         |
+| `test` / `test:cov` / `test:integration` | Vitest + integration suites                                                                      |
+| `create-package <name>`                  | Scaffold a new `packages/<name>/` workspace from `./create-package.sh`                           |
 
-The `create-package` script allows you to create a new package within the `packages` directory. It automates the setup of a new package with the necessary directory structure and initial files scaffolded.

+# `defi-app`
 
-Everything you need to build a Svelte project, powered by [`sv`](https://github.com/sveltejs/cli).
+SvelteKit product frontend for the DEFI.COM app. It is a client-heavy PWA-style app mounted under the SvelteKit base path `/app`, with the repo-root route redirecting users to `/v2` inside that base path.
 
-## Creating a project
+## Package shape
 
-If you're seeing this, you've probably already done this step. Congrats!
+| Item               | Current state                                                                               |
+| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Package name       | `defi-app`                                                                                  |
+| Framework          | SvelteKit 2 + Svelte 5                                                                      |
+| Build tool         | Vite 7                                                                                      |
+| Runtime adapter    | `@sveltejs/adapter-node`                                                                    |
+| Base path          | `/app` from `svelte.config.js`                                                              |
+| App-page rendering | SSR disabled and CSR enabled by route page options                                          |
+| Styling            | Tailwind CSS Vite plugin + `@deficom/ui-kit/styles`                                         |
+| State/query layer  | TanStack Svelte Query plus Svelte 5 runes modules                                           |
+| Monitoring         | Sentry SvelteKit plugin, client hook, server hook, and server instrumentation               |
+| I18n               | Paraglide via `project.inlang`                                                              |
+| Local proxy        | Vite proxies `/api` and `/indexer` to `PROXY_BACKEND`, defaulting to `https://nlayer.group` |
+| Test runner        | Vitest node project for `src/**/*.{test,spec}.{js,ts}`                                      |
+| Container image    | `apps/defi-app/Dockerfile`, `oven/bun:1`, `turbo build --filter=defi-app...`                |
+| Azure deploy       | Built as the `frontend` image and updated last in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`            |
+
+## Commands
 
 ```sh
-# create a new project in the current directory
-npx sv create
+# Start the Vite dev server for the frontend workspace.
+bun --filter defi-app dev
 
-# create a new project in my-app
-npx sv create my-app
-```
+# Run SvelteKit sync and svelte-check.
+bun --filter defi-app check
 
-## Developing
+# Run ESLint.
+bun --filter defi-app lint
 
-Once you've created a project and installed dependencies with `npm install` (or `pnpm install` or `yarn`), start a development server:
+# Run Vitest once. The script passes through to `test:unit`.
+bun --filter defi-app test
 
-```sh

-npm run dev -- --open

 ```
 
-## Building
+For end-to-end local development with Docker infrastructure, Prisma migrations, Rust services, Ponder, and the frontend together, use the repo-level `bun run dev:stack`.
+
+## Source map
+
+| Path                                    | Role                                                                                                                                                     |
+| --------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `src/routes/`                           | SvelteKit route tree. The root `+page.ts` redirects to `/v2`; product pages live under `v2/`.                                                            |
+| `src/lib/components/`                   | Shared and flow-specific Svelte components, including activity, cards, KYC, modals, transaction preview/status, wallet, and UI primitives.               |
+| `src/lib/modules/`                      | Domain modules for passkeys, device vault, balances, tokens, stealth, send, swap, earn, invest, fiat, Payy, WalletConnect, onboarding, PIN, and pricing. |
+| `src/lib/query/` and `src/lib/queries/` | Query-key factories and Svelte query wrappers for fiat, Rain, tokens, transactions, stealth, DeFi ID, and related mutations.                             |
+| `src/lib/config/`                       | Frontend environment wrapper, RPC helpers, and stealth chain/token configuration.                                                                        |
+| `src/lib/server/`                       | Server-only SvelteKit helpers; currently includes the Zerion proxy client used by `/api/zerion/transactions`.                                            |
+| `src/lib/monitoring/`                   | Sentry environment selection and breadcrumb scrubbing helpers.                                                                                           |
+| `static/`                               | Manifest, icons, add-to-home assets, and other static media.                                                                                             |
+| `src/service-worker.ts.disabled`        | Historical service-worker implementation kept disabled; it is not registered by the current app.                                                         |
+
+## Routes
+
+The app has 37 tracked page/server route entry files. Public URLs are served below the `/app` base path in deployed environments, while route files are listed here in SvelteKit route notation.
+
+| Area                        | Routes                                                                                                                                 |
+| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Redirect and health         | `/` redirects to `/v2`; `/health` is a server health endpoint.                                                                         |
+| App shell                   | `/v2` plus `/v2/+layout.*` for auth, PIN, device-vault hydration, background stealth scanning, and tab navigation.                     |
+| Onboarding and identity     | `/v2/onboarding`, `/v2/onboarding/create`, `/v2/onboarding/finish`, `/v2/onboarding/id-setup`.                                         |
+| Fiat and KYC                | `/v2/kyc`, `/v2/bank/deposit`, `/v2/bank/withdraw`.                                                                                    |
+| Rain cards                  | `/v2/cards`, `/v2/cards/details`, `/v2/cards/fund`, `/v2/cards/transactions`, `/v2/cards/withdraw`, plus legacy `/account/rain/apply`. |
+| Transfers and receive       | `/v2/send`, `/v2/send/select-asset`, `/v2/deposit`, `/v2/deposit/[userEns]`.                                                           |
+| DeFi and markets            | `/v2/earn`, `/v2/earn/deposit`, `/v2/invest`, `/v2/invest/[symbol]`, `/v2/swap`, `/v2/swap/select-asset`.                              |
+| Stealth                     | `/v2/stealth/setup`, `/v2/stealth/inbox`.                                                                                              |
+| Activity and contacts       | `/v2/activity`, `/v2/contacts`.                                                                                                        |
+| Settings                    | `/v2/settings`, `/v2/settings/pin`, `/v2/settings/external-connections`.                                                               |
+| Developer/internal surfaces | `/v2/tx-preview`, `/v2/tx-status-demo`, `/sentry-example-page`.                                                                        |
+| Same-origin API             | `/api/zerion/transactions` proxies Zerion activity through a SvelteKit server endpoint so the browser does not hold the Zerion key.    |
+
+## Environment
+
+`src/lib/config/env.ts` is the canonical frontend wrapper for:
+
+- `PUBLIC_ZERODEV_PROJECT_ID`
+- `PUBLIC_PASSKEY_SERVER_URL`
+- `PUBLIC_FIAT_SERVER_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:3001`)
+- `PUBLIC_TRADFI_SERVER_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:3002`)
+- `PUBLIC_ENSO_API_TOKEN`
+- `PUBLIC_ZEROEX_API_KEY`
+- `PUBLIC_GRAPH_TOKEN_API_KEY`
+- `PUBLIC_ALCHEMY_API_KEY`
+- `PUBLIC_YIELD_XYZ_API_KEY`
+- `PUBLIC_SUBGRAPH_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:42069/graphql`)
+- `PUBLIC_PNL_INDEXER_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:42070`)
+- `PUBLIC_PAYY_RPC_URL`
+- `PUBLIC_PAYY_USDC_ADDRESS`
+- `PUBLIC_PAYY_VAULT_REGISTRY_ADDRESS`
+
+Other env surfaces:
+
+| Variable                                      | Where it is used                                                                                                                                                      |
+| --------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `PUBLIC_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID`             | Read directly from `$env/dynamic/public` by the WalletConnect connection manager.                                                                                     |
+| `ZERION_API_KEY`                              | Server-only key for `src/lib/server/zerion.ts` and the same-origin `/api/zerion/transactions` route.                                                                  |
+| `ZERION_BASE_URL`                             | Optional server-only Zerion base URL override.                                                                                                                        |
+| `PROXY_BACKEND`                               | Vite dev-server target for `/api` and `/indexer`; defaults to `https://nlayer.group`.                                                                                 |
+| `PUBLIC_BUNDLER_URL` / `PUBLIC_PAYMASTER_URL` | Present in `.env.example` for operator context, but not read by `src/lib/config/env.ts`; current bundler/paymaster URLs are derived from `PUBLIC_ZERODEV_PROJECT_ID`. |
 
-To create a production version of your app:
+Production builds fail fast if `PUBLIC_SUBGRAPH_URL` contains banned development targets such as `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, or `api.studio.thegraph.com`.
+
+## Internal integrations
+
+`defi-app` depends on most workspace TypeScript packages because it is the composition layer:
+
+| Package                               | Frontend use                                                                                    |
+| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `@deficom/config`                     | Supported chains, chain metadata, explorer URLs, DeFi ID constants, RPC mapping.                |
+| `@deficom/passkeys`                   | Hybrid passkey authentication service and PRF support checks.                                   |
+| `@deficom/common`                     | Shared account/client types.                                                                    |
+| `@deficom/account`                    | Kernel account DeFi client actions and swap quote helpers.                                      |
+| `@deficom/defi-id`                    | DeFi ID availability and modal flows.                                                           |
+| `@deficom/defi-aggregator`            | DeFi pool/position data, PnL facade, quotes, Zerion provider, and transaction/stealth adapters. |
+| `@deficom/iron`                       | Iron autoramp transaction and blockchain types for fiat flows.                                  |
+| `@deficom/rain`                       | Rain card PIN encryption helpers referenced by Rain query flows.                                |
+| `@deficom/stealth-address-sdk`        | Stealth meta-address, payment scan, and stealth address generation helpers.                     |
+| `@deficom/kernel-stealth-address-sdk` | Kernel stealth claim and aggregate swap/transfer execution helpers.                             |
+| `@deficom/tokens`                     | Unified token balance types and token facade usage.                                             |
+| `@deficom/tokenlists`                 | Browser-safe token list entrypoint and bundled chain token lists.                               |
+| `@deficom/decentralised-icons`        | Token icon lookup.                                                                              |
+| `@deficom/ui-kit`                     | Shared Svelte UI components and global styles.                                                  |
+
+External service integrations include passkey-server, fiat-server, tradfi-server, the announcement indexer GraphQL endpoint, ZeroDev, Alchemy, Graph Token API, Enso, Yield.xyz, WalletConnect, Payy, Sumsub, Zerion, Iron, and Rain. See `docs/architecture.md` for the end-to-end service diagrams and privacy boundaries.
+
+## Deployment
+
+`apps/defi-app/Dockerfile` builds the frontend image with Bun:
+
+1. copy the monorepo into an `oven/bun:1` builder;
+2. run `bun install --ignore-scripts`;
+3. run `bunx --bun turbo build --filter=defi-app...`;
+4. copy `node_modules`, `packages`, `apps/defi-app/build`, and package metadata into the runtime stage;
+5. run `bun run build/index.js` as user `bun` on port `3000`.
+
+The reusable `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` matrix builds the `frontend` image from this Dockerfile alongside `passkey-server`, `fiat-server`, and `announcement-indexer`. During deploy, Container Apps are updated backend-first and the `frontend` Container App is updated last so the UI does not point at stale backend revisions.
+
+## Tests and checks
+
+The package currently has 28 tracked `src/**/*.{spec,test}.*` files. The Vitest config defines a `server` project in node environment for TypeScript specs and leaves browser/Svelte component tests disabled because they require Playwright installation.
+
+Useful package-level checks:
 
 ```sh
-npm run build
+bun --filter defi-app check
+bun --filter defi-app lint
+bun --filter defi-app test
+bun --filter defi-app build
 ```
 
-You can preview the production build with `npm run preview`.
+The package's `test` script delegates to `test:unit` with `--run --passWithNoTests`; use the Bun workspace wrapper from the repo root so the command runs in the current monorepo toolchain.
+
+## Cross-references
 
-> To deploy your app, you may need to install an [adapter](https://svelte.dev/docs/kit/adapters) for your target environment.

+- [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) sections 9.2 and 9.3 — deploy workflow and Dockerfile shape.
+- [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) section 13.1 — frontend env surfaces and Sentry notes.
+- [Top-level README](../../README.md) — repo-wide package and service inventory.

+# `@deficom/docs`
+
+Private Vocs documentation site for the repo's curated developer docs. The package lives under `apps/docs/`, is included by the root `apps/*` workspace glob, and is not a deployed Azure Container App on this branch.
+
+## Package shape
+
+| Item                                      | Current state                                             |
+| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Package name                              | `@deficom/docs`                                           |
+| Package type                              | Private ESM package                                       |
+| Docs engine                               | Vocs                                                      |
+| UI runtime deps                           | `react`, `react-dom`, `vocs`                              |
+| Package dev deps                          | `glob`, `gray-matter`                                     |
+| Dev / preview port                        | `1243` in `vocs.config.ts`                                |
+| TypeScript config                         | Includes `vocs.config.ts` and `docs/**/*`; `strict: true` |
+| Package-local test/lint/typecheck scripts | None                                                      |
+
+## Commands
+
+```sh
+# Start the Vocs dev server on port 1243.
+bun --filter @deficom/docs dev
+
+# Regenerate tracked LLM text outputs only.
+bun --filter @deficom/docs generate:llms
+
+# Generate LLM outputs, then build the static Vocs site.
+bun --filter @deficom/docs build
+
+# Preview the built Vocs site on port 1243.
+bun --filter @deficom/docs preview
+```
+
+The package does not define `test`, `lint`, `check-types`, or `typecheck` scripts. Root-level Turbo commands may include this workspace only for tasks that exist in `apps/docs/package.json`.
+
+## Source and generated files
+
+Tracked files are split into these groups:
+
+| Path                           | Role                                                                    |
+| ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `vocs.config.ts`               | Vocs title, description, top navigation, sidebar, and dev/preview port. |
+| `docs/pages/**/*.mdx`          | Authored docs pages.                                                    |
+| `scripts/generate-llms-txt.ts` | Reads `docs/pages/` and writes the tracked LLM text files.              |
+| `docs/public/llms.txt`         | Generated page index for LLM clients.                                   |
+| `docs/public/llms-full.txt`    | Generated concatenation of all authored docs pages.                     |
+
+`docs/dist/` and `.turbo/` are ignored build/cache output. Do not treat them as source-of-truth for this app; rebuild them from the tracked sources when needed.
+
+## Page inventory
+
+The Vocs sidebar currently exposes:
+
+| Section      | Pages                                                                                               |
+| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Home         | `/` from `docs/pages/index.mdx`                                                                     |
+| Guides       | `/guides/getting-started`, `/guides/rust-dev-setup`, `/guides/running-locally`                      |
+| Architecture | `/architecture/stealth-addresses`, `/architecture/smart-accounts`, `/architecture/indexer-pipeline` |
+| Contracts    | `/contracts/overview`, `/contracts/deployed-addresses`                                              |
+
+## LLM outputs
+
+`scripts/generate-llms-txt.ts` recursively collects Markdown and MDX pages from `docs/pages/`, sorts them by URL path, and writes:
+
+-   `docs/public/llms.txt` — grouped links to each page's `.md` URL, with `/` mapped to `/llms-full.txt`.
+-   `docs/public/llms-full.txt` — all page content concatenated into one Markdown document.
+
+If a source page changes, rerun `bun --filter @deficom/docs generate:llms` and commit both generated public files when they change.
+
+## Deployment status
+
+`apps/docs` is a workspace docs site, not a production service in the current Azure deploy workflow. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` builds and updates the frontend, passkey server, fiat server image, optional fiat-server worker, and announcement indexer; it does not deploy `@deficom/docs`.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/pages/index.mdx`](./docs/pages/index.mdx) — docs-site homepage.
+-   [`vocs.config.ts`](./vocs.config.ts) — navigation and sidebar configuration.
+-   [`scripts/generate-llms-txt.ts`](./scripts/generate-llms-txt.ts) — LLM output generator.
+-   [Top-level README](../../README.md) — workspace-wide setup and current repo layout.
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — source-backed architecture overview for the broader repo.

+# `@deficom/tradfi-server`
+
+Private Bun + Hono API used by the frontend TradFi investing screens. It serves tokenized-stock and tokenized-gold metadata, on-chain wrapper prices, Alpha Vantage market data, and DefiLlama-backed chart data from `apps/tradfi-server/`.
+
+> Current status: the TypeScript source is present and the frontend actively calls it, but compose/deploy support is only partially recovered. `apps/tradfi-server/Dockerfile` is not tracked, while `docker-compose.passkey.yml` and `nginx.conf` still reference a `tradfi-server` container.
+
+## Package shape
+
+| Item                | Current state                                                                            |
+| ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Package name        | `@deficom/tradfi-server`                                                                 |
+| Runtime             | Bun                                                                                      |
+| HTTP framework      | Hono                                                                                     |
+| Default port        | `3002`                                                                                   |
+| Source size         | 8 tracked TypeScript files under `src/`; 1,056 lines                                     |
+| Workspace role      | Private app workspace; not a library package                                             |
+| TypeScript config   | Extends `@deficom/config-typescript/base.json`, includes only `src/**/*`                 |
+| Start commands      | `bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server dev` or `bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server start` |
+| Placeholder scripts | `build`, `check-types`, and `lint` currently print `skipped`                             |
+
+## Environment
+
+`src/lib/env.ts` reads four variables:
+
+| Variable                | Default                               | Use                                                                                                                            |
+| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `PORT`                  | `3002`                                | Bun server listen port.                                                                                                        |
+| `CORS_ORIGIN`           | `*`                                   | Hono CORS origin.                                                                                                              |
+| `ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY` | empty string                          | API key passed to `AlphaVantageClient`. Empty is accepted at startup, but Alpha Vantage-backed requests may fail at call time. |
+| `ETH_RPC_URL`           | `https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com` | Mainnet RPC used for ERC-20 `totalSupply` reads.                                                                               |
+
+The frontend uses `PUBLIC_TRADFI_SERVER_URL`, exposed through `ENV.TRADFI_SERVER_URL`, to call this service. `apps/defi-app/.env.example` points it at `http://localhost:3002`.
+
+## Data sources
+
+| Source                         | Wiring                                                         | Used for                                                                 |
+| ------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
+| `@deficom/config` token tables | `TOKENIZED_STOCKS`, `TOKENIZED_GOLD`, symbol maps              | Static tokenized stock/gold metadata and provider tokens.                |
+| `@deficom/stocks`              | `AlphaVantageClient`, `AlphaVantageProvider`                   | Quotes, fundamentals, movers, news, gold spot, and gold history parsing. |
+| `@deficom/defi-aggregator`     | DefiLlama client + pricing facade with `DefiLlamaPriceAdapter` | Token wrapper prices, stock sparklines, and stock history charts.        |
+| `viem` mainnet client          | `createPublicClient({ chain: mainnet })`                       | ERC-20 `totalSupply` reads for tokenized-provider market caps.           |
+
+## Routes
+
+`src/app.ts` mounts `/health`, `/api/stocks`, and `/api/gold`.
+
+| Method | Path                                                        | Purpose                                                                                                                                                          |
+| ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `GET`  | `/health`                                                   | Health probe returning `{ status: "ok", service: "tradfi-server" }`.                                                                                             |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks`                                               | Lists tokenized stocks with provider metadata and live on-chain prices; filters out rows with no live provider price.                                            |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/sparklines?range=1W\|1M\|3M`                   | Batched DefiLlama close-price series for list-page sparklines.                                                                                                   |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/movers`                                        | Alpha Vantage top gainers, losers, and most-active symbols.                                                                                                      |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol`                                       | Stock detail with Alpha Vantage quote, provider token data, preferred provider, and tokenized-market-cap enrichment.                                             |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/quote`                                 | Quote-only response for one symbol.                                                                                                                              |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/history?range=1D\|1W\|1M\|3M\|1Y\|ALL` | DefiLlama chart data for the preferred tokenized wrapper. The response is not exchange OHLCV; OHLC fields are filled from wrapper spot prices and volume is `0`. |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/overview`                              | Alpha Vantage company overview.                                                                                                                                  |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/earnings`                              | Alpha Vantage earnings data.                                                                                                                                     |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/news`                                  | Alpha Vantage news sentiment feed.                                                                                                                               |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/income-statement`                      | Alpha Vantage income statement.                                                                                                                                  |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/balance-sheet`                         | Alpha Vantage balance sheet.                                                                                                                                     |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/cash-flow`                             | Alpha Vantage cash flow.                                                                                                                                         |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/dividends`                             | Alpha Vantage dividends.                                                                                                                                         |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/splits`                                | Alpha Vantage splits.                                                                                                                                            |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/insider-transactions`                  | Alpha Vantage insider transactions.                                                                                                                              |
+| `GET`  | `/api/stocks/:symbol/etf-profile`                           | Alpha Vantage ETF profile.                                                                                                                                       |
+| `GET`  | `/api/gold`                                                 | Lists tokenized gold assets with provider token data.                                                                                                            |
+| `GET`  | `/api/gold/:symbol`                                         | Gold detail with provider data and live spot price where available.                                                                                              |
+| `GET`  | `/api/gold/:symbol/spot`                                    | Spot-price-only gold response.                                                                                                                                   |
+| `GET`  | `/api/gold/:symbol/history?interval=daily\|weekly\|monthly` | Alpha Vantage gold/silver history converted through `AlphaVantageProvider`.                                                                                      |
+
+## Cache behavior
+
+The stock route module uses process-local `SimpleCache` instances. Gold routes do not use these caches: `/api/gold` returns static config data, while gold detail, spot, and history call Alpha Vantage per request.
+
+| Data                                         | TTL                                       |
+| -------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
+| Stock quotes                                 | 60 seconds                                |
+| Wrapper spot prices                          | 30 seconds                                |
+| ERC-20 total supply                          | 10 minutes                                |
+| Preferred provider                           | 10 minutes                                |
+| Stock history / sparklines                   | 1 hour, except `1D` history at 60 seconds |
+| Company overview, earnings, and fundamentals | 24 hours                                  |
+| Top movers                                   | 5 minutes                                 |
+
+Expired stock-route cache entries are cleaned every 5 minutes.
+
+## Frontend consumer
+
+`apps/defi-app/src/lib/query/tradfi.keys.ts` is the main consumer. It calls:
+
+-   `/api/stocks`, `/api/stocks/sparklines`, and `/api/stocks/:symbol`
+-   `/api/stocks/:symbol/{overview,earnings,news,income-statement,balance-sheet,cash-flow,dividends,splits,insider-transactions}`
+-   `/api/stocks/:symbol/history?range=...`
+-   `/api/gold`, `/api/gold/:symbol`, and `/api/gold/:symbol/history`
+
+Current page consumers include the invest list, invest detail, and dashboard tokenized-stock enrichment paths. A `goldWithDetails` query is defined in the query factory, but no page imports it directly on this branch.
+
+## Local commands
+
+```sh
+# Start the Bun server.
+bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server dev
+
+# Current package scripts for build/typecheck/lint are placeholders.
+bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server build
+bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server check-types
+bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server lint
+
+# Run Vitest if specs are added under this app.
+bun --filter @deficom/tradfi-server test
+```
+
+There are no tracked test files under `apps/tradfi-server/` at the time of this README.
+
+## Deployment status
+
+This app is not part of the main Azure deploy workflow on this branch. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` builds and updates the frontend, passkey server, fiat server image, optional fiat-server worker, and announcement indexer; it does not build a TradFi container app.
+
+Local compose references also are not currently runnable for this service without restoring a Dockerfile:
+
+-   `docker-compose.passkey.yml` defines a `tradfi-server` service with `dockerfile: apps/tradfi-server/Dockerfile`.
+-   `nginx.conf` defines `tradfi_backend` as `tradfi-server:3002` and routes `tradfi.seikai.app` to it.
+-   `apps/tradfi-server/Dockerfile` is absent from the tracked tree.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`apps/defi-app/src/lib/query/tradfi.keys.ts`](../defi-app/src/lib/query/tradfi.keys.ts) — frontend query keys and endpoint consumers.
+-   [`packages/stocks/`](../../packages/stocks/) — Alpha Vantage client and parsers.
+-   [`packages/defi-aggregator/`](../../packages/defi-aggregator/) — DefiLlama client, pricing facade, and token-chart helpers.
+-   [`packages/config/`](../../packages/config/) — tokenized stock/gold metadata.
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — app-state note describing the partial recovery and Dockerfile gap.

+# `crates/db`
+
+Cargo workspace member providing the **PostgreSQL repository layer** for the Rust services. SQLx-backed (compile-time query verification via the `.sqlx/` offline cache committed at the repo root). Schema is owned by Prisma in [`packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma`](../../packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma); this crate reads that schema, plus two boot-time idempotent migration helpers the Rust services apply at every startup. They have **different roles**: `migrate_claimed_at` makes an existing Prisma migration (`packages/database/prisma/migrations/20260323000000_add_claimed_at`) idempotent at service boot via `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, so the column gets re-applied if the service starts against a Postgres that hasn't been Prisma-migrated yet (the dev-stack does this; production runs Prisma first). `migrate_iron_state` creates the Iron state projection tables (`iron_customer_state`, `iron_autoramps`, `iron_fiat_addresses`) plus indexes plus a `DO $$ BEGIN … END $$` backfill block — these tables are **not** in the Prisma schema and have no Prisma migration.
+
+Library only — no binaries. Used by `services/fiat-server` (with the `openapi` feature for utoipa schemas) and `services/passkey-server` (default features). For the system context see [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §6.2 (crates table) and §7 (Prisma models + migrations).
+
+## Cargo
+
+```toml
+[dependencies]
+sqlx, uuid, chrono, serde, serde_json, thiserror, tracing
+utoipa = { workspace = true, optional = true }   # behind feature "openapi"
+
+[features]
+openapi = ["dep:utoipa"]
+```
+
+The `openapi` feature gates `#[cfg_attr(feature = "openapi", derive(utoipa::ToSchema))]` on **6 of the 16** model structs — the ones that cross the fiat-server HTTP boundary (`UserIronMapping`, `UserRainMapping`, `RainTransaction`, `RainUserComplianceStatus`, `SumsubApplicant`, `DeFiIdEntry`). The other 10 structs (e.g. `PasskeyUser`, `IronWebhookEvent`, `IronCustomerState`) don't derive it because they're either internal-only or not currently surfaced in the openapi spec. Pulled by `services/fiat-server/Cargo.toml:23` (`db = { path = "../../crates/db", features = ["openapi"] }`); not pulled by passkey-server.
+
+## Module layout (`src/`)
+
+```text
+lib.rs      Re-exports + 1 readiness probe (`ping`) + 2 idempotent boot migrations.
+error.rs    `Error` enum (sqlx::Error wrapper + `NotFound`) — re-exported as `db::Error`.
+models.rs   16 public structs covering Passkey, Iron, Rain, Sumsub, DeFi-ID domains. Re-exported via `db::*`. 15 are SQLx row types (`#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]`); 1 is `RainTransactionUpsert`, an input DTO that derives only `Debug, Clone, Default`.
+pool.rs     `create_pool` / `create_pool_with_config` / `PoolConfig`. Re-exported.
+defi_id.rs  DeFi-ID entry CRUD + filter queries. 5 pub async fns.
+iron.rs     User mappings (5 fns) + idempotency keys (4 fns: create, get, complete, cleanup-expired) + webhook events (7 fns: create, get-by-id, get-unprocessed for live + replay, mark-processed, try-claim, cleanup-expired) + Iron state projections (10 fns: customer KYC + lifecycle status, autoramp status + reads, fiat-address status + reads, customer/autoramp/fiat-address snapshot upserts). 26 pub async fns total — the largest per-domain module.
+passkey.rs  Passkey user CRUD (3 fns: create, get-by-id, get-latest-by-username) + credential CRUD (5 fns: create, get-by-id, get-by-user, prf-salt update, counter update). 8 pub async fns total.
+rain.rs     User mappings + webhook events (create + reads + processing + claim/cleanup) + transaction projection upserts + compliance status reads. 18 pub async fns total.
+sumsub.rs   Applicant lifecycle (init upsert, lookups by user/external/applicant id, review update, share-token, mark-rain-submitted) + webhook events (create, idempotency checks, replay queries). 12 pub async fns total.
+```
+
+## Public API surface
+
+Everything imported as `db::<thing>`. The lib root re-exports:
+
+```rust
+pub use error::Error;          // db::Error
+pub use models::*;             // PasskeyUser, IronWebhookEvent, RainTransaction, SumsubApplicant, DeFiIdEntry, …
+pub use pool::create_pool;     // db::create_pool(database_url) -> PgPool
+pub use pool::create_pool_with_config;
+pub use pool::PoolConfig;
+pub use sqlx::PgPool;          // db::PgPool — no need to depend on sqlx directly
+```
+
+Per-domain functions stay namespaced (`db::passkey::create_user`, `db::iron::create_user_mapping`, `db::rain::create_webhook_event`, `db::sumsub::upsert_applicant_init`, `db::defi_id::find_many_with_filters`).
+
+### Models (16 public structs in `models.rs` — 15 SQLx row types + 1 input DTO)
+
+| Domain  | Structs                                                                                                             |
+| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| Passkey | `PasskeyUser`, `PasskeyCredential`                                                                                  |
+| Iron    | `UserIronMapping`, `IronIdempotencyKey`, `IronWebhookEvent`, `IronCustomerState`, `IronAutoramp`, `IronFiatAddress` |
+| Rain    | `UserRainMapping`, `RainWebhookEvent`, `RainTransaction`, `RainTransactionUpsert`, `RainUserComplianceStatus`       |
+| Sumsub  | `SumsubApplicant`, `SumsubWebhookEvent`                                                                             |
+| DeFi-ID | `DeFiIdEntry`                                                                                                       |
+
+These mirror the Prisma model names where the Prisma model exists (`PasskeyUser`, `PasskeyCredential`, `UserIronMapping`, `IronIdempotencyKey`, `IronWebhookEvent`, `UserRainMapping`, `RainWebhookEvent`, `SumsubApplicant`, `SumsubWebhookEvent`, `DeFiIdEntry`); the `IronCustomerState` / `IronAutoramp` / `IronFiatAddress` family is created by `lib.rs::migrate_iron_state` and is not in Prisma; `RainTransaction` is backed by Prisma migration `20260415120000_add_rain_transactions` (raw-SQL) but doesn't appear in the Prisma model file. `RainTransactionUpsert` is an in-memory input DTO used as the argument to upsert calls, not a row mapped from the database.
+
+### Lib-root helpers
+
+| Function             | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
+| -------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `ping(&PgPool)`      | Readiness probe — issues `SELECT 1` to exercise connection + auth. Used by service `/health` handlers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
+| `migrate_claimed_at` | Idempotent boot helper — re-applies the existing Prisma migration `20260323000000_add_claimed_at` via `ALTER TABLE iron_webhook_events / rain_webhook_events ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS claimed_at TIMESTAMP(3)`. Safe re-run if fiat-server starts against a Postgres that hasn't been Prisma-migrated yet (the dev-stack flow). Powers the 180-second stale-claim scheduler in `services/fiat-server/src/services/scheduler.rs`. |
+| `migrate_iron_state` | Idempotent boot migration — creates the `iron_customer_state`, `iron_autoramps`, `iron_fiat_addresses` projection tables (with indexes), plus a `DO $$ BEGIN … END $$` backfill for per-field timestamp columns on pre-existing rows. **Not** in the Prisma schema and has no Prisma migration — this crate is the source of truth for these tables.                                                                             |
+
+Both migrations run at every fiat-server boot (`services/fiat-server/src/main.rs:43-50`) and are safe to re-run.
+
+## SQLx offline mode
+
+This crate uses `sqlx::query!` / `query_as!` / `query_scalar!` macros throughout (verified at compile time against the schema). The repo root commits the cached query metadata under `.sqlx/`, so `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check -p db` works without a live database — that's what CI does.
+
+When you **add or modify** a query macro in this crate, you must regenerate the offline cache or CI's offline build will fail on the new query:
+
+```bash
+# DATABASE_URL must point at a Postgres instance whose schema matches your macros.
+# Run from the repo root.
+cargo sqlx prepare --workspace
+git add .sqlx/
+```
+
+A plain `cargo build` with `DATABASE_URL` set validates queries online but does **not** write the cache — `prepare` is the regeneration step. See the SQLx invariant in [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) for the full rule.
+
+## Build & test
+
+```bash
+# Type-check from the workspace root (uses .sqlx/ offline cache).
+SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check -p db
+
+# Build (same offline guarantee).
+SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo build -p db
+
+# With openapi feature (matches what fiat-server pulls).
+SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check -p db --features openapi
+```
+
+There are **no in-crate tests** (`crates/db/` has only `Cargo.toml` + `src/`). Coverage of these functions lives in the consumer services' integration tests (`services/fiat-server/tests/iron_webhook_integration.rs`, `rain_webhook_integration.rs`, `cache_integration.rs`, `shared_lock.rs`, `services/passkey-server/tests/smoke.rs`, `authenticator.rs`) which exercise the repository layer end-to-end against a live Postgres from `bun run dev:stack`.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §6.2 crates table, §7 Prisma models + migrations, §13 surprising-things (Redis DB-1 split, compile-time DB binding)
+-   [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Repository Invariants (especially the SQLx offline-mode rule)
+-   [`packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma`](../../packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma) — schema source of truth (Prisma owns; SQLx reads)
+-   [`packages/database/prisma/migrations/`](../../packages/database/prisma/migrations/) — Prisma migration history (12+ migrations)
+-   [`services/fiat-server/`](../../services/fiat-server/) — primary consumer (with `openapi` feature)
+-   [`services/passkey-server/`](../../services/passkey-server/) — secondary consumer

+# `crates/iron-client`
+
+Cargo workspace member providing a typed Rust HTTP client for the **Iron** API. Production host is `iron.xyz`; sandbox base URL (the value passed to `IronClientConfig::base_url`) is `https://api.sandbox.iron.xyz/api` per `CLAUDE.md` Repository Invariants and `services/fiat-server/src/config.rs:126-127`. The legacy `getiron.com` host has been dead since 2026-04-08. Library only — no binaries. Used by `services/fiat-server` (with the `openapi` feature for utoipa schemas) and indirectly by anything fiat-server proxies (`apps/defi-app/src/lib/...` via the FE → fiat-server `/api/iron/*` route family).
+
+For the system context see [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) §1.1 (system diagram), §5.2 (fiat-server endpoint inventory — Iron rows), §11.1 (trust boundaries — outbound auth: API-key header, NOT HMAC; HMAC is for inbound webhooks per `services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs`), and §13 surprising-things (Iron host invariant).
+
+## Cargo
+
+```toml
+[dependencies]
+reqwest, serde, serde_json, uuid, thiserror, tracing
+utoipa = { workspace = true, optional = true }   # behind feature "openapi"
+
+[features]
+openapi = ["dep:utoipa"]
+```
+
+The `openapi` feature gates `#[cfg_attr(feature = "openapi", derive(utoipa::ToSchema))]` only on the shared types in `src/types.rs` (Customer, Identification, Signing, the autoramp/address enum family, …). Per-module request DTOs in `addresses.rs`, `autoramps.rs`, `currencies.rs`, and `webhooks.rs` (e.g. `RegisterFiatAddressRequest`, `ListAutorampsParams`, `ExchangeRateParams`, `UpdateWebhookRequest`) do **not** derive `ToSchema` — they're argument-shape DTOs that fiat-server doesn't currently surface in its OpenAPI spec. Pulled by `services/fiat-server/Cargo.toml:24` (`iron-client = { path = "../../crates/iron-client", features = ["openapi"] }`).
+
+## Module layout (`src/`)
+
+```text
+lib.rs           Re-exports — IronClient, IronClientConfig, IronError, types::*. 10 pub modules (addresses, autoramps, client, currencies, customers, error, sandbox, transactions, types, webhooks).
+client.rs        IronClient struct — Arc<reqwest::Client> with rustls TLS + 15s timeout.
+                 IronClientConfig (api_key + base_url + optional sub_partner_id).
+                 build_query_path — manual URL-param encoding (handles &, =, #, +, space, %).
+                 IronClient::request<T> — common request pipeline (X-API-Key header,
+                 optional IDEMPOTENCY-KEY, optional X-Sub-Partner-Id).
+error.rs         IronError enum (re-exported as iron_client::IronError).
+types.rs         Shared request/response types (Customer, Identification, Signing, autoramp
+                 enums, etc.) re-exported via iron_client::*.
+addresses.rs     AddressesClient — register self-hosted/hosted wallets, fiat addresses.
+autoramps.rs     AutorampsClient — quotes, limits, autoramps, transactions.
+currencies.rs    CurrenciesClient — exchange rates.
+customers.rs     CustomersClient — customer CRUD + identifications + signings.
+sandbox.rs       IronSandboxClient — sandbox-only flow helpers (gated downstream by
+                 fiat-server's ENABLE_SANDBOX env, see services/fiat-server/README.md).
+transactions.rs  TransactionsClient — transaction reads.
+webhooks.rs      WebhooksClient — outbound webhook subscription management
+                 (CRUD on Iron's webhook records — distinct from inbound webhook
+                 verification, which lives in services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs).
+```
+
+## Public API surface
+
+Everything imported as `iron_client::<thing>`. The lib root re-exports:
+
+```rust
+pub use client::{IronClient, IronClientConfig};
+pub use error::IronError;
+pub use types::*;     // CustomerType, AddressFilter, Customer, Identification, …
+```
+
+The 7 sub-clients (`AddressesClient`, `AutorampsClient`, `CurrenciesClient`, `CustomersClient`, `IronSandboxClient`, `TransactionsClient`, `WebhooksClient`) stay namespaced under their modules. Typical usage:
+
+```rust
+let cfg = IronClientConfig {
+    api_key: "...".into(),
+    base_url: "https://api.sandbox.iron.xyz/api".into(),  // sandbox; see config.rs:126-127
+    sub_partner_id: None,
+};
+let client = IronClient::new(cfg);
+let customers = client.customers();   // -> CustomersClient
+let new_customer = customers.create(req).await?;
+```
+
+(See `services/fiat-server/src/state.rs` for the actual instantiation pattern.)
+
+### Auth (outbound)
+
+Every request carries `X-API-Key: <api_key>` set by `IronClient::request` per `src/client.rs:75`. No HMAC, no signing — that's the inbound-webhook side, handled by `services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs` (standardwebhooks crate + 5-minute `TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECS` + `event_id` uniqueness). This split is the doc-line-item that codex iter-20 corrected on `architecture.md` §11.1 — outbound is API-key-only; HMAC is inbound only.
+
+Idempotent endpoints additionally take an `IDEMPOTENCY-KEY` header (set per-call). Sub-partner routing uses the optional `X-Sub-Partner-Id` header from `config.sub_partner_id`.
+
+### TLS + timeout
+
+`reqwest::Client::builder().use_rustls_tls().timeout(Duration::from_secs(15))`. No Native TLS dependency. 15-second hard timeout per request — Iron's slowest sandbox endpoints sometimes brush this; production is well under.
+
+### Manual URL encoding
+
+`build_query_path(base, &[(k, v)])` does its own minimal percent-encoding for `& = # + space %` because reqwest's default `Url::query_pairs_mut` would re-encode some Iron-side allowed chars unpredictably. If you add a new query-string call site, use `build_query_path` rather than `reqwest::Url::query_pairs_mut`.
+
+## Build & test
+
+```bash
+# Type-check from the workspace root.
+cargo check -p iron-client
+
+# With openapi feature (matches what fiat-server pulls).
+cargo check -p iron-client --features openapi
+
+# Build.
+cargo build -p iron-client
+```
+
+There are **no in-crate tests** (`crates/iron-client/` has only `Cargo.toml` + `src/`). Unlike `crates/db`, this crate doesn't even ship example doctest fixtures yet. Coverage of the client paths lives in `services/fiat-server/tests/iron_webhook_integration.rs` and the live Iron sandbox / nlayer runs (manual + the `auto_webhook_sync` Job — see `docs/architecture.md` §9.2).
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §6.2 crates table, §11.1 trust boundaries (outbound vs inbound auth split), §13 surprising-things (Iron host invariant)
+-   [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — Repository Invariants (Iron host = `iron.xyz`; legacy `getiron.com` dead since 2026-04-08)
+-   [`services/fiat-server/`](../../services/fiat-server/) — primary consumer (Iron + Sumsub + Rain proxy; with `openapi` feature)
+-   [`services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs`](../../services/fiat-server/src/routes/iron_webhooks.rs) — inbound webhook handler (HMAC + 5-min tolerance + event_id uniqueness; **not** part of this crate)
+-   [`packages/iron/`](../../packages/iron/) — TypeScript package (separate FE-facing library; this crate is the Rust server-side counterpart)

+# `crates/mq/`
 
-Message queue abstraction with compile-time backend selection via Cargo feature flags.
+Message queue abstraction with compile-time backend selection via Cargo feature flags. **Three backends, mutually exclusive, no default.** Only `azqueue` is selected by any consumer in this workspace; `nats` and `servicebus` are dead-code paths kept for optionality.
+
+For full context (the live-vs-dead status of each backend, the consumer pin, the dlq-drain binary's place in the fiat-server image, and the known infra `-dlq`/`-poison` mismatch) see [`README.md`](./README.md).
 
 ## Files
 
-| File                          | What                                                                                                                                | When to read                                             |
-| ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
-| `src/lib.rs`                  | Feature-gated module exports; conditionally re-exports NATS or Service Bus implementations under the same public names              | Understanding which symbols are available per feature    |
-| `src/streams.rs`              | Queue/stream name constants and delivery parameter constants (ACK_WAIT_SECS, MAX_DELIVER, IRON_WEBHOOKS_QUEUE, RAIN_WEBHOOKS_QUEUE) | Changing queue names or delivery tuning                  |
-| `src/types.rs`                | `WebhookMessage` and `WebhookSource` types (backend-agnostic)                                                                       | Adding message fields                                    |
-| `src/client.rs`               | NATS JetStream client (feature = "nats")                                                                                            | Debugging NATS connection issues                         |
-| `src/consumer.rs`             | NATS JetStream consumer (feature = "nats")                                                                                          | Debugging NATS consumption                               |
-| `src/publisher.rs`            | NATS JetStream publisher (feature = "nats")                                                                                         | Debugging NATS publish                                   |
-| `src/servicebus_client.rs`    | Azure Service Bus AMQP client (feature = "servicebus")                                                                              | Debugging Service Bus connection                         |
-| `src/servicebus_consumer.rs`  | Service Bus PeekLock consumer with lock renewal (feature = "servicebus")                                                            | Debugging Service Bus consumption, lock expiry           |
-| `src/servicebus_publisher.rs` | Service Bus publisher; `subject_to_queue` maps NATS-style subjects to queue names (feature = "servicebus")                          | Debugging publish, understanding subject mapping         |
-| `src/bin/dlq_drain.rs`        | CLI for operator-triggered DLQ inspection and replay (feature = "servicebus")                                                       | Responding to DLQ alerts, replaying dead-letter messages |
-| `Cargo.toml`                  | Feature flag declarations: `nats` (default), `servicebus`; optional dependencies                                                    | Changing features or adding dependencies                 |

+
+The constants in `streams.rs` split into **two groups**: live shared params used by all three backends, and NATS-only stream retention limits not used by the live `azqueue` path or by `servicebus`. +**Live shared params** (used by all 3 backends):
+
+| Constant        | Value | Meaning                                                                 |
+| --------------- | ----- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `MAX_DELIVER`   | 5     | Max delivery attempts before poison routing.                            |
+| `ACK_WAIT_SECS` | 30    | Visibility timeout / lock duration. Handlers exceeding this must renew. |
+
+**NATS-only retention** (not imported by `azqueue_*` or `servicebus_*` modules):
+
+| Constant       | Value  | Meaning                                                                                                  |
+| -------------- | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `MAX_AGE_SECS` | 7 days | NATS stream retention age. Service Bus retention is per-queue in IaC; Queue Storage uses Azure defaults. |
+| `MAX_BYTES`    | 1 GiB  | NATS stream byte cap. Not used by Service Bus or Queue Storage paths.                                    |
+
+The fiat-server webhook handlers are idempotent (DB-first write + UNIQUE-constraint guards) — duplicate delivery on lock expiry does not cause duplicate side effects (architecture.md §11.1).

+## Why the abstraction exists
+
+Originally NATS JetStream for local dev + Service Bus for production (compile-time-swap pattern, ref DL-001 / DL-002). +The `azqueue` backend was added later as the actual production target — Queue Storage HTTP REST is cheaper and simpler than Service Bus, with a poison-queue convention layered in the application instead of relying on Service Bus's native DLQ.

@@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+# `crates/rain-sdk/`
+
+**Vendored upstream crate.** Local copy of [`rain-sdk` 1.2.0](https://crates.io/crates/rain-sdk/1.2.0) (upstream `https://github.com/yezz123/rain-rust-sdk`), pulled into the workspace so we can ship patches without waiting on upstream releases.
+
+This is **not** a our-code crate — `Cargo.toml` is the auto-generated form Cargo emits when packaging for crates.io (note the leading "THIS FILE IS AUTOMATICALLY GENERATED BY CARGO" header). When you read or edit this directory, you're working with vendored third-party code with two local deltas applied on top.
+
+## Two-document setup
+
+| File                             | Source                | What it covers                                                                                                                                                             |
+| -------------------------------- | --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| [`README.md`](./README.md)       | Upstream (verbatim)   | The SDK reference — features, install, quick-start, the `RainClient` + `Config` + `Environment` + `AuthConfig` API surface, feature flags (`async`, `axum`, `rustls-tls`). |
+| [`VENDORING.md`](./VENDORING.md) | Local (this monorepo) | Why we vendored, the two deltas vs upstream 1.2.0, and the upgrade procedure.                                                                                              |
+| [`CHANGELOG.md`](./CHANGELOG.md) | Upstream (verbatim)   | Upstream release notes through 1.2.0.                                                                                                                                      |
+
+When you read this directory, **start with `VENDORING.md`** for the monorepo context, then fall back to upstream `README.md` for SDK reference. The upstream README has several **known drifts** from the vendored tree — see "Known upstream-README drifts" below before treating any specific README claim as accurate for our copy.
+
+## Known upstream-README drifts
+
+`README.md` is upstream verbatim from the published `rain-sdk` 1.2.0 release. We don't rewrite vendored upstream docs, but several README claims are out of sync with this vendored copy. Verified iter 52 against current source:
+
+1. **`sync` feature claim is broken** (README L21, L36-40, L185-187 advertise sync support). `cargo check -p rain-sdk --features sync` fails:
+
+    ```text
+    crates/rain-sdk/src/client.rs:507:32 unresolved import `reqwest::blocking::header`
+    crates/rain-sdk/src/api/disputes.rs:223:24 method `build_url` is private
+    ```
+
+    Only the `async` feature (and the default no-feature build) compiles. The fiat-server consumer pulls `async`, so this doesn't affect production — but the README implies sync is a valid choice and it isn't.
+
+2. **`signup_consumer` example doesn't typecheck** after our `ApplicationStatus::NotStarted` vendor patch. README L111-125 tells users to run `cargo run --example signup_consumer --features async`, which fails:
+
+    ```text
+    error[E0004]: non-exhaustive patterns in examples/signup_consumer.rs:185
+    match arm missing for ApplicationStatus::NotStarted
+    ```
+
+    Direct consequence of the local delta documented in `VENDORING.md` (the upstream example was written against upstream 1.2.0, which doesn't have the variant).
+
+3. **Stale upstream packaging/links** in README:
+    - **Version**: install snippets at L33 + L40 say `rain-sdk = { version = "0.1.0", ... }`. Actual `Cargo.toml:14` is `version = "1.2.0"`. The 0.1.0 wording is upstream-historic.
+    - **Feature list**: README L191 lists a `json` feature ("JSON serialization support, enabled by default"). No such feature exists in `Cargo.toml` (`[features]` block declares only `async`, `axum`, `rustls-tls`, `sync`, `default`).
+    - **Doc links**: README L195-197 links to `./docs/signup.md`, `./docs/managing_users.md`, `./docs/managing_cards.md`. None of those files exist under `crates/rain-sdk/` in this vendored copy. Same for `LICENSE` (L7 + L201).
+
+## What to trust
+
+The upstream `README.md` is reliable for: the high-level API shape (`RainClient` / `Config` / `Environment` / `AuthConfig`), feature-flag enumeration **with the caveats above**, the link to upstream `docs.rs/rain-sdk` for full type-level docs, and the architectural framing.
+
+For anything that touches the local copy specifically (compilable feature flags, runnable examples, version pin, in-tree links), trust this `CLAUDE.md` + `VENDORING.md` over the upstream README.
+
+## Local deltas (verbatim from `VENDORING.md`)
+
+1. **`src/models/common.rs:69-72` — `ApplicationStatus::NotStarted`.** The Rain dev API returns `"notStarted"` for freshly created applications; upstream 1.2.0 is missing the variant and every status read fails to deserialize. The added variant carries an inline `// VENDOR PATCH:` comment explaining why.
+2. **`src/client.rs:193-211` — `build_url` strips `?query`/`#fragment` before `path_segments_mut`.** Upstream 1.2.0 URL-encodes `?` as `%3F`, which breaks every endpoint that takes query params (`list_cards`, `list_users`, `list_companies`, etc.). The fix carries an inline `// VENDOR PATCH:` comment.
+
+Both deltas are necessary for the live `services/fiat-server` Rain proxy to work correctly today; reverting either breaks that path.
+
+## Consumer
+
+The only consumer in this workspace is **`services/fiat-server/Cargo.toml:25`** via `rain-sdk = { workspace = true }`. The workspace pin is declared at root `Cargo.toml:57` as `rain-sdk = { path = "crates/rain-sdk" }`.
+
+`services/fiat-server` uses `rain-sdk` for outbound calls to the Rain Cards API (issuance, transactions, KYC bridge into Sumsub via the Rain compliance flow). The corresponding **inbound** webhook handler is `services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain_webhooks.rs` — that uses raw HMAC + signed-timestamp freshness from the request body and is independent of this SDK (the SDK is outbound-only).
+
+## Context
+
+This crate **replaced** an earlier `crates/rain-client` that was deleted in commit `a54c3905` (2026-03-23) along with the legacy JS servers under `apps/fiat-server` and `apps/passkey-server` — see top-level [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) and the `crates/` row in its Subdirectories table for the historical note.
+
+Architecture.md §6.2 references this crate by name; §11.1 documents that the **outbound** Rain auth uses `Api-Key` header (handled by this crate at `src/auth.rs`), distinct from the inbound webhook HMAC-SHA256 verification in `services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain_webhooks.rs`.
+
+## Build & test
+
+Standard Cargo invocations. The `Makefile` and `taplo` config (locally vendored) carry upstream tooling that's not wired into our workspace CI — our `.github/workflows/rust.yml` builds this crate transitively via `services/fiat-server`.
+
+```sh
+# Type-check from the workspace root.
+cargo check -p rain-sdk
+
+# With the async feature (matches what fiat-server pulls).
+cargo check -p rain-sdk --features async
+```
+
+The `examples/` directory is upstream sample code; it's `autoexamples = false` in the auto-generated `Cargo.toml` so the workspace doesn't try to build them.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+-   [`README.md`](./README.md) — upstream SDK reference (verbatim from the published crate)
+-   [`VENDORING.md`](./VENDORING.md) — vendoring rationale + delta list + upgrade procedure
+-   [`CHANGELOG.md`](./CHANGELOG.md) — upstream release notes
+-   [`docs/architecture.md`](../../docs/architecture.md) — §6.2 crates table, §11.1 trust boundaries (outbound `Api-Key` here vs inbound HMAC in fiat-server)
+-   [top-level `CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) — historical note on the `crates/rain-client` → `crates/rain-sdk` swap (`a54c3905`)
+-   [`services/fiat-server/`](../../services/fiat-server/) — the only consumer; uses `rain-sdk` for outbound Rain API calls
+-   [`services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain_webhooks.rs`](../../services/fiat-server/src/routes/rain_webhooks.rs) — inbound Rain webhook handler (raw HMAC + signed-timestamp freshness; not part of this SDK)
